name: The Rosarito Reservoir from Rosalejo — circular by the Tiétar irrigation channels
name_original: El Embalse de Rosarito desde Rosalejo — circular por los Canales de riego del Tiétar
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Rosalejo are characterized by a tranquil environment and scenic landscapes, offering a blend of natural beauty and quiet roads. The region features the Tiétar River and the Sierra de Gredos mountain range, providing a picturesque backdrop for rides. Cyclists can expect routes that often cross numerous irrigation canals and offer views of the rising Sierra de Gredos. The area provides dedicated cycling infrastructure, including quiet routes like the Acequia del Tiétar Road, ensuring an engaging cycling experience. Best road cycling routes around Rosalejo The most popular road cycling route is Belvís de Monroy from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular via Bohonal de Ibor and the Embalse de Valdecañas, a 32.4 miles (52.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain and passes by the Embalse de Valdecañas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pórtico de Curia de Talavera la Vieja desde Navalmoral De La Mata — circular por la Presa del Embalse de Valdecañas, a moderate 33.8 miles (54.4 km) path. This route offers views of the Presa del Embalse de Valdecañas and includes varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Talayuela from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular by Tiétar, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through the Tiétar River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Rosalejo is defined by the presence of the Tiétar River, views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains, and numerous irrigation canals. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Rosalejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 450 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Rosalejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Rosalejo?
A: Rosalejo offers a diverse network of road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from &lt;em&gt;easy&lt;/em&gt; to &lt;em&gt;moderate&lt;/em&gt; and even &lt;em&gt;difficult&lt;/em&gt;, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist. In total, there are 5 distinct routes available.

Q: What is the typical terrain and elevation like on road cycling routes in Rosalejo?
A: The terrain around Rosalejo is characterized by its scenic landscapes, often featuring views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains and numerous irrigation canals. While some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353175443/talayuela-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-by-tietar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talayuela from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular by Tiétar&lt;/a&gt;, have moderate elevation gains around 169 meters, others, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353259887/belvis-de-monroy-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-via-bohonal-de-ibor-and-the-embalse-de-valdecanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvís de Monroy from Navalmoral de la Mata — circular via Bohonal de Ibor and the Embalse de Valdecañas&lt;/a&gt;, can feature significant elevation gains of nearly 690 meters, offering more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in the Rosalejo area?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route suitable for a more relaxed ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353230255/talayuela-tietar-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talayuela - Tiétar River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is classified as easy, covering approximately 47.6 kilometers with an elevation gain of around 240 meters, making it a good option for families or those seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Rosalejo offer stunning natural scenery. You'll frequently encounter the &lt;strong&gt;Tiétar River&lt;/strong&gt; and enjoy picturesque views of the rising &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Gredos&lt;/strong&gt; mountains. Many routes also cross numerous irrigation canals, adding to the unique landscape. A notable feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia del Tiétar Road&lt;/a&gt;, known for its quiet, motor-traffic-restricted environment and beautiful surroundings.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Rosalejo?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Rosalejo are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353259887/belvis-de-monroy-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-via-bohonal-de-ibor-and-the-embalse-de-valdecanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvís de Monroy from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353266333/portico-de-curia-de-talavera-la-vieja-desde-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-por-la-presa-del-embalse-de-valdecanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pórtico de Curia de Talavera la Vieja desde Navalmoral De La Mata&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353185596/the-rosarito-reservoir-from-rosalejo-circular-by-the-tietar-irrigation-channels" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Rosarito Reservoir from Rosalejo&lt;/a&gt;, all offering scenic circular journeys.

Q: What is the quality of the road surface like for cycling in Rosalejo?
A: The region is known for its quiet and well-maintained roads, particularly the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia del Tiétar Road&lt;/a&gt;, which boasts a good road surface ideal for smooth rides. Cyclists can generally expect favorable conditions for road biking throughout the area.

Q: What towns or amenities are accessible along the road cycling routes?
A: While Rosalejo itself is tranquil, the broader area offers potential stops. The rural town of Talayuela, located in the Tiétar River valley, is a possible refreshment stop, as seen in routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353175443/talayuela-from-navalmoral-de-la-mata-circular-by-tietar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talayuela from Navalmoral de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;. Navalmoral de la Mata, a larger rural farming town, is also centrally located and could serve as a hub for exploring the area and finding amenities.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Rosalejo?
A: The road cycling routes in Rosalejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil environment, scenic landscapes, and the blend of natural beauty with quiet roads, especially highlighting the views of the Sierra de Gredos mountains and the Tiétar River.

Q: What is the most challenging road cycling route in Rosalejo?
A: For those seeking a significant challenge, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353185596/the-rosarito-reservoir-from-rosalejo-circular-by-the-tietar-irrigation-channels" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Rosarito Reservoir from Rosalejo — circular by the Tiétar irrigation channels&lt;/a&gt; is classified as a difficult route. It spans approximately 78.3 kilometers and features an elevation gain of around 345 meters, offering a demanding ride.

Q: Is there a particularly quiet road for cycling in Rosalejo?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia del Tiétar Road&lt;/a&gt; is specifically noted for being forbidden to most motorized traffic. This ensures a quiet and safe environment, making it an ideal choice for cyclists looking for a peaceful ride through beautiful surroundings.

Q: What makes Rosalejo a unique destination for road cycling?
A: Rosalejo stands out for its tranquil environment, scenic landscapes, and dedicated cycling infrastructure. The region offers a unique blend of natural beauty with the Tiétar River and Sierra de Gredos as a backdrop, combined with quiet, well-maintained roads like the Acequia del Tiétar Road, which minimizes traffic interference and enhances the cycling experience.
tips: Massive network of irrigation canals moving the waters of the Tiéter River from the Rosarito Reservoir throughout this area. Great cycle route away from cars and traffic.
The reservoir that collects the waters of the Tiétar River. The reservoir constitutes the geographical border between La Mancha and Castilla y León. Stunning views from the dam.

This road is forbidden to most motorized traffic. It is very quiet, and the surroundings are beautiful. Good road surface too. This is just the segment of the road that I cycled during my route, but do some research, you will find the whole road.
